whyMatters: Esta noticia representa un desarrollo relevante en su sector.
fullArticle:
Welcome to the 345th installment of A View From the Easel, a series in which artists reflect on their workspace. This week, Adam de Boer fashions lush scenes on canvas using Indonesian batik-dyed fabric, which he waxes and boils himself.

Want to take part? Check out our submission guidelines and share a bit about your studio with us through this form! All mediums and workspaces are welcome, including your home studio.

How long have you been working in this space?

I usually arrive by 8:30am and spend the first 30 to 45 minutes catching up on emails and social media correspondence. For the next 10 hours, I work on my batik paintings (but with a short teatime break in the mid-afternoon). Over the past few years, most studio work has been for exhibitions, and to prepare for those, I usually...
